title: Implement codex-kernel-guard as scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh (bash, executable). This is the Phase 1 Kernel control-plane router. Read these files first: - .codex/prompts/kernel.md (full contract) - docs/kernel/requirements-freeze-v1.md (frozen spec) - CODEX.md (Codex role and boundary) Commands to...
summary_text: Implemented [scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh). It is executable, uses `set -euo pipefail`, keeps mutations routed through the existing `scripts/lib/kernel-*.sh` layer, and adds the missing read-only `doctor` surface over compact artifacts, receipt status, runtime health, shared secrets, and bounded run detail. The requested command set is wired, with a small compatibility `glm-reset` passthrough kept because the existing test matrix references it. Validation: `bash -n scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh` passed. The exact requested `bash tests/test-codex-kernel-guard-doctor.sh` could not run in this workspace because that test hardcodes `/Users/masayuki_otawara/bin/codex-kernel-guard`, which does not exist here. I ran the same test body against the new repo script by substituting the guard path, and it passed: `codex kernel guard doctor check passed`.
